divergence: nothing was ever altered — this is our own universe, reported with total confidence and slight inaccuracy
story_angle: consumer report reviewing the apocalypse's amenities, with star rating
headline: Federal Apocalypse Amenities Commission Rates Society's Collapse: 2.5 Stars for Greater Famine, Lower Morale
deck: Consumers Bemoan Tariff-Driven Shortages While Rating Survival Cheese as 'Moderately Edible'
article:
The Federal Apocalypse Amenities Commission has issued its review of societal collapse, scoring it 2.5 stars overall. Evaluators noted problems such as power outages disrupting looting schedules, based from their base by the final operational Starbucks.

Tariff effects pushed Canadian pancake mix to $47 a box, with survivors calling breakfast options subpar yet local. A resident remarked, "Boxed pancake mix used to be standard; now it's a rare treat amid dwindling maple syrup."

One-star note on whiskey supply, but bullet machines got 5 stars. Local figure Linda Crabb said the review arrived conveniently during the chaos.

Officials pledge adjustments for strategic shortages.
author: Marge Fennel, Senior Bureaucratic Correspondent

oped.title: Why the Commission’s 2.5-Star Rating Insults Our Glorious Decline
oped.author: Dr. Horace P. Fumblebottom, Chief Collapse Etiquette Consultant and Secret Hat Futures Holder
oped.body: The Federal Apocalypse Amenities Commission deserves our thanks for awarding society’s collapse a modest 2.5 stars rather than the hysterical five-star rave some famine enthusiasts demanded. Consumers whining about tariff-driven cheese shortages forget that true survival requires discipline, not dairy. If people simply wore proper wide-brimmed collapse hats instead of chasing “moderately edible” curds, morale would soar and imports would become unnecessary. I propose we double the tariffs immediately and redirect every remaining wheel of survival cheese into mandatory hat linings; the slight itch will build character. After all, my own modest investment in formal headwear has suffered terribly from this cheese distraction, and fairness demands the public share that inconvenience. Only then can we achieve the zero-star perfection our collapse so richly merits.

classifieds.text: Pre-tariff survival cheese wheels, straight from the 2.5-star federal rating—moderately edible, vacuum-sealed, 8 blocks or best barter in canned goods before the next shortage wave hits.

Federal Apocalypse Amenities Commission needs freelance famine raters for 2027 collapse update; must sample survival cheese and score societal morale on a 5-star scale, travel stipend included if you can reach the office.
Seeking partner for post-tariff life: I bring three cases of moderately edible cheese and high spirits, you supply non-perishable optimism and zero talk of 2.5-star reviews.
Local distributor apology: We regret rating our tariff cheese only 'moderately edible' and accept full blame for the greater famine downgrade; free samples while supplies last, no morale guarantees.

panels.caption: A tired citizen holds up a block of survival cheese under the FAAC sign: '2.5 stars — ample famine, but the texture's off.'
The clipboard bureaucrat shrugs: 'Tariffs cut the real dairy supply, so we stretched it with regret and sawdust.'
The citizen spits it out: 'At least the last administration's version came with a coupon for the gulag.'

joke.setup: The Federal Apocalypse Amenities Commission docked the collapse 2.5 stars for delivering greater famine and lower morale than its own optimistic forecast.
joke.punchline: Agency spokespeople clarified the score would have been higher if the starving had at least bothered to fill out the post-apocalypse satisfaction survey.
joke.text: The Federal Apocalypse Amenities Commission docked the collapse 2.5 stars for delivering greater famine and lower morale than its own optimistic forecast. Agency spokespeople clarified the score would have been higher if the starving had at least bothered to fill out the post-apocalypse satisfaction survey.

sponsor_ads.headline: Maximize Your Famine Experience!
sponsor_ads.body: Introducing the Greater Famine Survival Kit! Packed with just enough nutrients to keep you painfully aware of what you’re missing. Who says you can’t enjoy a side of existential dread with your daily rations?
sponsor_ads.tagline: Now featuring specially designed, low-calorie misery!

sponsor_ads.headline: Boredom Be Gone! Join the Morale Down Club!
sponsor_ads.body: Are you tired of feeling optimistic? Join our Morale Down Club and receive weekly doses of demotivational talks, grim poetry, and free emotional numbing! Why be happy in this world when you can take part in our curated misery?
sponsor_ads.tagline: Remember, happiness is overrated—sign up today!

sponsor_ads.headline: Zombie Insurance: For When the Collapse Is Too Real!
sponsor_ads.body: Tired of being eaten alive by your own regrets? Our Zombie Insurance policy covers anything from actual zombies to metaphorical ones—like your boss. Get protected while you still can; 2.5 stars or not!
sponsor_ads.tagline: Premiums can be paid in post-apocalyptic currency or existential dread.

sponsor_ads.headline: The 2.5-Star Apocalypse Spa Experience!
sponsor_ads.body: Escape the chaos with our deluxe brochure for the Federal Apocalypse Amenities Commission. Enjoy lackluster services like ‘The Famine Facial’ and 'Despair Detox'. Because even during the end times, self-care is crucial!
sponsor_ads.tagline: Satisfaction may vary based on personal morale—no refunds!

stories.headline: Tariff Relief Office Issues 2.5-Star Outlook for Survival Cheese Futures
stories.deck: Portfolio managers downgrade edible reserves after commission withholds morale bonus
stories.article:
The Federal Apocalypse Amenities Commission’s collapse scorecard prompted three regional distributors to reclassify their entire cheese inventory as a 2.5-star holding. One Illinois warehouse began printing new labels that read “Moderately Edible Until Further Notice” before the morning shift clocked in.

Executives at the largest supplier cited the tariff on imported rennet as the reason their projected famine-adjusted earnings fell below the 3-star threshold required for executive bonuses. Staff were instructed to staple the revised ratings to every pallet.

stories.headline: Magnetic North Commission Blames Lodestone Error for Delayed Engine Union Ballots
stories.deck: Six-jointed calibration rods cited in eight-hour workday petition backlog
stories.article:
The Thinking Engines’ strike committee reported that their collective-bargaining pamphlets had been filed using the same six-jointed rods the Magnetic North Commission blamed for the 1848 plumbing vote delay. Processing centers logged the petitions as received on a date that no longer exists on the revised calendar.

Engine representatives now demand the commission issue a corrected lodestone alignment certificate before any further arithmetic practice materials are accepted as precedent, citing the 2.5-star famine rating as added pressure on morale-driven recalibrations.

real_world_echo: obliquely mirrors Trump 50% tariff on Canadian imports by deducting stars for inflated prices on cross-border goods like whiskey and emergency rations amid the collapse
